title: eupatorium cruciatum
intro: Eupatorium cruciatum, also known as Joe-Pye weed, is a perennial herbaceous plant native to North America, characterized by its tall stature and clusters of small, fragrant flowers. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties due to the presence of sesquiterpene lactones and flavonoids. The health benefits of Joe-Pye weed include its ability to alleviate symptoms of fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues, as well as its use as an antimalarial agent. Joe-Pye weed is commonly prepared as a tea, tincture, or infusion, and can also be used in poultices and salves to treat skin conditions and wounds.
